paper_title: Pollen Morphology Study of Leea Species

methods.description: Process for preparing pollen samples from Leea species for microscopic analysis, including fixation, acetolysis, and slide preparation.

steps.description: Fresh polliniferous materials from the collected specimen were fixed directly in glacial acetic acid.
steps.searchable_summary: fix pollen samples in glacial acetic acid

steps.description: Pollen preparations were made by the acetolysis method standardized by Erdtman (1952) and modified by Nair (1970).
The acetolysed pollen were stored in 1:1 glycerine and distilled water.
steps.searchable_summary: store acetolysed pollen in glycerine and water

steps.description: Permanent slides for light microscopic studies were prepared by mounting the acetolysed pollen in glycerine jelly and sealing with paraffin wax.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare permanent slides with glycerine jelly and paraffin wax

methods.description: Microscopic examination of pollen samples using light microscopy (LM) and scanning electron microscopy (SEM) to study morphological features.

steps.description: LM photomicrographs of the pollen preparations were taken using an Olympus CH-20 Research Microscope with Digital camera attachment facility.
SEM pictures were taken at the NIIST, Pappanamcode, Trivandrum using a JEOL-JSM-5600LV scanning electron microscope.
Morphological features of pollen grains including those relating to the aperture, exine ornamentation, pollen size and shape were studied from LM and SEM observations.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen morphology from LM and SEM

methods.description: Measurement of quantitative and qualitative palynological characters of pollen grains using microscopy.

steps.description: The value of P/E ×100 was found in the case for ascertaining the shape class, following the pollen shape classification suggested by Erdtman (1966).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate P/E ratio for pollen shape classification

steps.description: Pollen size classes were ascertained following Walker & Doyle (1975).

The quantitative pollen traits (polar and equatorial pollen diameter, P/E ratio, exine thickness and aperture number) were measured using an ocular micrometer, and in each case, the mean values of the measurements were taken from a random sample of 30 pollen grains.
steps.searchable_summary: measure quantitative pollen traits using ocular micrometer

steps.description: Qualitative palynological characters studied include pollen shape, type, size class, exine ornamentation, mural nature (shape, wall, width and height), luminal nature (depth, floor, shape and wax plugging) and aperture shape.
